about.description: The story of Andrés Santana: from musician to Software Developer. Automation, development and AI training from Colombia.

intro.p1: Before writing code, I wrote songs. Music was the life plan — until technology transformed the industry and forced me to choose between what I loved and what let me make a living. I chose to survive.
intro.p2: I learned to code from scratch and took it seriously: for over 4 years I was the frontend lead at Mexico's fastest-growing digital pharmacy. I cut load time by 56%, lifted conversions 23%, and the platform I architected processes $2M+ monthly for 50,000+ patients with 99.9% uptime.
intro.p3: Along the way I found my unfair advantage: I can do the technical work and I can translate it. Because I lived the fear of falling behind, I learned to teach technology from empathy, not arrogance.
intro.p4: Today I work on three fronts: AI automation for companies, custom software development, and AI training for non-technical professionals.
intro.p5: My north star is simple: help professionals master AI before the world forces them to choose. It forced me. It doesn't have to force you.
intro.title: My story

about.label: About

aws.description: Deploying and scaling frontend infrastructure on AWS. Experience with S3 + CloudFront for static assets, Lambda@Edge for dynamic rendering, and Amplify for full-stack deployments with CI/CD integration.
aws.title: AWS

cicd.description: Automated pipelines with GitHub Actions, Vercel, and AWS. Feature branch previews, automated testing gates, Lighthouse CI budgets, and zero-downtime deployments for production applications.
cicd.title: CI/CD & DevOps

graphql.description: Designing and consuming GraphQL APIs with Apollo Client and codegen for fully typed queries. Real-time subscriptions for live data feeds, optimistic UI updates, and efficient cache management for complex financial data.
graphql.title: GraphQL

performance.description: Systematic approach to Core Web Vitals: code-splitting, lazy loading, image optimization (WebP/AVIF), critical CSS inlining, and caching strategies. Proven track record reducing LCP by 56% on high-traffic platforms.
performance.title: Performance Optimization

react.description: Building production applications with React 18+ and Next.js App Router. Experienced with Server Components, Streaming, Parallel Routes, and ISR for high-performance, SEO-optimized frontends.
react.title: React / Next.js

tailwind.description: Utility-first styling with Tailwind CSS for rapid, consistent UI development. Custom design system tokens, responsive layouts, and component-level theming integrated with design tools like Figma.
tailwind.title: Tailwind CSS

typescript.description: Strict TypeScript across the full stack. Type-safe API contracts, generic utility types, and end-to-end type safety from database schemas to UI components. Reduces runtime errors and accelerates team velocity.
typescript.title: TypeScript

xstate.description: Modeling complex UI flows with finite state machines using XState. Ideal for multi-step checkout processes, payment flows, and approval workflows where predictable state transitions are critical.
xstate.title: State Management (XState)

about.title: About me

achievements.1: Delivered 10+ projects for startups and SMBs across e-commerce, SaaS, and real estate industries.
achievements.2: Built end-to-end web platforms from Figma designs to production deployment on Vercel.

achievements.1: Led frontend architecture for Mexico's fastest-growing digital pharmacy, serving 50,000+ patients with chronic conditions.
achievements.2: Reduced LCP from 5.8s to 2.5s (-56%), directly improving conversion rates by 23%.
achievements.3: Architected modular checkout system processing $2M+ monthly in transactions with 99.9% uptime.
achievements.4: Led migration from legacy React to Next.js 15 with zero downtime, reducing bundle size by 40%.

hackathon.description: 5-person team, 30 non-stop hours, 70 builders competing — at the first hackathon in Santa Marta's history.
hackathon.title: 3rd place — Primera Hackatón Samatech

story.imageAlt: Engineer reviewing production system dashboards in a dark space lit by emerald light
story.p1: I led the frontend of Mexico's fastest-growing digital pharmacy: a platform processing $2M+ per month for 50,000 patients at 99.9% uptime. Real engineering, in production, for over 6 years.
story.p2: That foundation is the difference when I talk about AI: no demos, no hype — I ship workflows to production and teach with the empathy of someone who knows what keeping up costs. That's why my program works for non-technical people.
story.title: 6+ years shipping software to production. Now, AI.

home.subline: I'm Andrés Santana, an applied AI expert. I've spent 6+ years building production software — a platform processing $2M+/month for 50K+ users — and today I teach non-technical professionals to automate their work through the AI Shift Challenge.
home.title: Andrés Santana — Applied AI Expert · AI Shift Challenge

1.answer: No. Everything is built with click-based tools you already know how to use. The program is designed for people whose job is not technology.
1.question: Do I need to know how to code?

2.answer: Four are private: you and me, on your role and your specific tasks. The other two are group sessions with the rest of the cohort, to see how other roles are solving it. All live, spread across the 4 weeks.
2.question: What are the sessions like?

3.answer: If your work runs through a computer — marketing, finance, HR, legal, healthcare, education, operations, sales — yes. Since most of the program is 1:1, everything is built on your real tasks, not generic examples.
3.question: Does it work for my field?

4.answer: The first cohort opens soon and there are only 6 spots — private sessions don't allow for more. Getting in is free: you start today with Week 0, and when the cohort opens you get in first, at launch pricing.
4.question: When does it start and how much does it cost?

5.answer: They will — that's why the program isn't married to any of them. You leave with workflows running today and a repeatable judgment system to evaluate whatever ships tomorrow.
5.question: What if the tools change next month?

automatizacion.description: You build your first automations with click-based tools — email, spreadsheets, documents — and free up hours every week. No code involved.
automatizacion.imageAlt: A professional's hands pulling back from the keyboard while the emerald-green screen keeps working
automatizacion.title: Your repetitive tasks, on autopilot

copiloto.description: From using AI ‘sometimes, sort of’ to a daily system: analyze information, write, decide and produce at professional quality in your field.
copiloto.imageAlt: Focused professional working at night with two screens casting cyan light on her face
copiloto.title: From loose prompts to workflows that deliver finished work

criterio.description: The tools will change. You leave with a repeatable method to evaluate what's new in minutes and adopt only what serves your work — for good.

criterio.title: A method to evaluate new tools in minutes

posicionamiento.description: Building it isn't enough: you finish knowing how to explain and demo your automation — to your boss, your team or your clients. The professional who solves with AI stands out.
posicionamiento.imageAlt: Professional presenting to their team, standing in front of a screen that bathes the room in cyan light

1.answer: AI process automation, adoption consulting and development with React, Next.js and TypeScript — especially platforms where performance and conversions matter.
1.question: What kind of projects do you take on?

2.answer: Yes. I've worked remotely with teams in Mexico, Colombia and the US for 5+ years, in English and Spanish, and I'm comfortable across time zones.
2.question: Do you work with international teams?

3.answer: A landing page typically takes 1-2 weeks; a full e-commerce build or migration, 6-12 weeks. After the discovery call you get a written proposal with a concrete timeline.
3.question: How long does a typical project take?

4.answer: Both. I can own the frontend end-to-end or integrate with your existing engineering team, including mentoring and code reviews.
4.question: Do you work solo or with teams?

5.answer: Project-based work is a fixed price agreed upfront. Retainers and consulting are billed monthly or by the hour. Every engagement starts with a free discovery call.
5.question: How does pricing work?
